trivia_bullets.text: Only printing; from Throne of Eldraine (ELD, October 2019), a fairy-tale plane overlaid on Arthurian legend. The clockwork gnome represents the castle's mechanical service infrastructure rather than any particular court's military. One of two colorless-common artifact golems in ELD alongside Henge Walker.

Adamant mechanic (draw a card if you spent at least three mana of the same color) was ELD's set mechanic rewarding mono-color devotion. As a colorless-cost artifact, Clockwork Servant can trigger Adamant with any single color's mana — agnostic to which court employs it. EDHREC rank 22,285. Not Pauper-legal (uncommon). Legal in Pioneer, Modern, Historic, Legacy, Commander.

"Some are annoyed by the constant ticking as they clean the cobbles, but to the castle custodians, there's no sweeter sound." — no named speaker. The unnamed custodians who find the ticking sweet are implicitly those who benefit from the labor without performing it.
Illustrated by David Palumbo, a prolific MTG illustrator known for detailed character work. No specific design commentary on this card has been located.
